text: Many experts predict that artificial intelligence will become far more advanced in the coming decades. A major goal is to create general AI, or Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Unlike current AI, which is good at specific tasks, AGI would be able to learn and perform any intellectual job that a human can do. The potential benefits are enormous, from solving complex medical problems to boosting economic productivity. However, there are also serious risks to manage, such as job displacement and the possibility of biased decision-making. While timeline predictions vary wildly, some believe we could see AGI within the next twenty to fifty years, while others think it may take much longer.
